author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXVII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Among all the Songs entitled the Song of degrees, this Psalm hath a further addition in the title, that it is “Solomon’s.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus it is entitled, “A Song of degrees of Solomon.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It hath therefore aroused our attention, and caused us to enquire the reason of this addition, “of Solomon.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is needless to repeat explanations of the other words, Song of degrees.…Solomon was in his time David’s son, a great man, through whom many holy precepts and healthful admonitions and divine mysteries have been wrought by the Holy Spirit in the Scriptures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Solomon himself was a lover of women, and was rejected by God: and this lust was so great a snare unto him, that he was induced by women even to sacrifice to idols, as Scripture witnesseth concerning him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if, by his fall what was delivered through him were blotted out, it would be judged that he had himself delivered these precepts, and not that they were delivered through him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The mercy of God, therefore, and His Spirit, excellently wrought that whatever of good was declared through Solomon, might be attributed unto God; and the man’s sin, unto the man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What marvel that Solomon fell among God’s people?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did not Adam fall in Paradise?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did not an angel fall from heaven, and become the devil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are thereby taught, that no hope must be placed in any among men.…The name of Solomon is interpreted to mean peacemaker: now Christ is the True Peacemaker, of whom the Apostle saith, “He is our Peace, who hath made both one.”…Since, therefore, He is the true Solomon; for that Solomon was the figure of this Peace maker, when he built the temple; that thou mayest not think he who built the house unto God was the true Solomon, Scripture showing unto thee another Solomon, thus commences this Psalm: “Except the Lord build the house, their labour is but lost that build it” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord, therefore, buildeth the house, the Lord Jesus Christ buildeth His own house.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many toil in building: but, except He build, “their labour is but lost that build it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are they who toil in building it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All who preach the word of God in the Church, the ministers of God’s mysteries.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are all running, we are all toiling, we are all building now; and before us others have run, toiled, and built: but “except the Lord build, their labour is but lost.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus the Apostles seeing some fall bewailed these men, in that they had laboured in vain for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We, therefore, speak without, He buildeth within.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We can observe with what attention ye hear us; He alone who knoweth your thoughts, knoweth what ye think.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He Himself buildeth, He Himself admonisheth, He Himself openeth the understanding, He Himself kindleth your understanding unto faith; nevertheless, we also toil like workmen; but, “except the Lord build,” etc.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that which is the house of God is also a city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the house of God is the people of God; for the house of God is the temple of God.…This is Jerusalem: she hath guards: as she hath builders, labouring at her building up, so also hath she guards.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this guardianship these words of the Apostle relate: “I fear, lest by any means your minds should be corrupted from the simplicity which is in Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was guarding the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He kept watch, to the utmost of his power, over those over whom he was set.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Bishops also do this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a higher place was for this reason given the Bishops, that they might be themselves the superintendents and as it were the guardians of the people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Greek word Episcopus, and the vernacular Superintendent, are the same; for the Bishop superintends, in that he looks over.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As a higher place is assigned to the vinedresser in the charge of the vineyard, so also to the Bishops a more exalted station is alloted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And a perilous account is rendered of this high station, except we stand here with a heart that causeth us to stand beneath your feet in humility, and pray for you, that He who knoweth your minds may be Himself your keeper.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since we can see you both coming in and going out; but we are so unable to see what are the thoughts of your hearts, that we cannot even see what ye do in your houses.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then can we guard you?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As men: as far as we are able, as far as we have received power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because we guard you like men, and cannot guard you perfectly, shall ye therefore remain without a keeper?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For where is He of whom it is said, “Except the Lord keep the city, the watchman waketh but in vain?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are watchful on our guard, but vain in our watchfulness, except He who seeth your thoughts guard you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He keepeth guard while ye are awake, He keepeth guard also whilst ye are asleep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He hath once slept on the Cross, and hath risen again; He no longer sleepeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be ye Israel: for “the Keeper of Israel neither sleepeth nor slumbereth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea, brethren, if we wish to be kept beneath the shadow of God’s wings, let us be Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we guard you in our office of stewards; but we wish to be guarded together with you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are as it were shepherds unto you; but beneath that Shepherd we are fellow-sheep with you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are as it were your teachers from this station; but beneath Him, the One Master, we are schoolfellows with you in this school.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we wish to be guarded by Him who was humbled for our sakes, and who was exalted to keep us, let us be humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no one assume anything unto himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No man hath any good, except he hath received it from Him who alone is good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he who chooseth to arrogate wisdom unto himself, is a fool.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him be humble, that wisdom may come, and may enlighten him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if, before wisdom cometh unto him, he imagine that he is wise; he riseth before light, and walketh in darkness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What doth he hear in this Psalm?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “It is but lost labour that ye haste to rise up before dawn” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If ye arise before light ariseth, ye must needs lose your labour, because ye will be in the dark.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our light, Christ, hath arisen; it is good for thee to rise after Christ, not to rise before Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who rise before Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who choose to prefer themselves to Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who are they who wish to prefer themselves to Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who wish to be exalted here, where He was humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them, therefore, be humble here, if they wish to be exalted there, where Christ is exalted.…The Lord recalled the sons of Zebedee to humility, and said unto them, “Are ye able to drink of the cup that I shall drink of?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I came to be humble: and are ye wishing to be exalted before Me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The way I go, do ye follow, He saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if ye choose to go this way where I do not go, your labour is lost, in rising before dawn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Peter too had risen before the light, when he wished to give the Lord advice, deterring Him from suffering for us.…But what did our Lord do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He caused him to rise after the Light: “Get thee behind Me, Satan.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was Satan, because he wished to rise before Light.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Get thee behind Me:” that I may precede, thou mayest follow: where I go, there thou mayest go; and mayest not wish to lead Me, where thou wouldest go.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as if thou shouldest say, When shall we rise? we are ordered now to sit: when will be our rising?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the Lord’s was.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look unto Him, who went before thee: for if thou heedest not Him, “it is lost labour for thee to rise before dawn.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When was He raised?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When He had died.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hope therefore for thine uplifting after thy death: have hope in the resurrection of the dead, because He rose again and ascended.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But where did He sleep?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On the Cross.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When He slept on the Cross, He bore a sign, yea, He fulfilled what had been signified in Adam: for when Adam was asleep, a rib was drawn from him and Eve was created; so also while the Lord slept on the Cross, His side was transfixed with a spear, and the Sacraments flowed forth, whence the Church was born.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Church the Lord’s Bride was created from His side, as Eve was created from the side of Adam.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But as she was made from his side no otherwise than while sleeping, so the Church was created from His side no otherwise than while dying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore He rose not from the dead save when He had died, dost thou hope for exaltation save after this life?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that this Psalm might teach thee, in case thou shouldest ask, When shall I rise? perhaps before I have sat down? he addeth, “When He hath given His beloved sleep” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God giveth this when His beloved have fallen asleep; then His beloved, that is, Christ’s, shall rise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all indeed shall rise, but not as His beloved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a resurrection of all the dead; but what saith the Apostle?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We shall all rise, but we shall not all be changed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They rise unto punishment: we rise as our Lord rose, that we may follow our Head, if we are members of Him.…Hope for such a resurrection; and for the sake of this be a Christian, not for the sake of this world’s happiness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou wish to be a Christian for the sake of this world’s happiness, since He thy Light sought not worldly happiness; thou art wishing to rise before the light; thou must needs continue in darkness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be changed, follow thy Light; rise where He rose again: first sit down, and thus rise, “when He giveth His beloved sleep.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if thou shouldest ask again, who are the beloved?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lo, children, the reward of the fruit of the womb, are an heritage of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since he saith, “fruit of the womb,” these children have been born in travail.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a certain woman, in whom what was said unto Eve, “in sorrow shalt thou bring forth children,” is shown after a spiritual manner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church beareth children, the Bride of Christ; and if she beareth them, she travaileth of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In figure of her, Eve was called also “the Mother of all living.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who said, “My little children, of whom I travail in birth again, until Christ be formed in you,” was amongst the members of her who travaileth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But she travailed not in vain, nor brought forth in vain: there will be a holy seed at the resurrection of the dead: the righteous who are at present scattered over the whole world shall abound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church groaneth for them, the Church travaileth of them; but in that resurrection of the dead, the offspring of the Church shall appear, pain and groaning shall pass away.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Like as the arrows in the hand of the mighty one, even so are the children of those that are shot out” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence hath sprung this heritage, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence hath sprung so numerous a heritage?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some have been shot out from the Lord’s hand, as arrows, and have gone far, and have filled the whole earth, whence the Saints spring.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this is the heritage whereof it is said, “Desire of Me, and I shall give thee the heathen for thine inheritance, and the uttermost parts of the earth for thy possession.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how doth this possession extend and increase unto the world’s uttermost parts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because, “like as the arrows in the hand of the mighty one,” etc. Arrows are shot forth from the bow, and the stronger the arm which hath sent it forth, the farther flieth the arrow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is stronger than the darting of the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From His bow He sendeth forth His Apostles: there could not be a spot left where an arrow shot by so strong an arm would not reach; it hath reached unto the uttermost parts of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The reason it went no farther was, that there were no more of the human race beyond.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He hath such strength, that even if there were a spot beyond, whither the arrow could fly, He would dart the arrow thither.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such are the children of those who are shot forth as they that are shot forth.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps the Apostles themselves are styled the sons of those who have been shaken out, the sons of the Prophets.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Prophets comprised closed and covered mysteries: they were shaken, that they might come forth thence manifestly.…Except the prophecy involved were sifted with diligence, would the concealed meanings come forth unto us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these meanings were therefore closed before the Lord’s advent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord came, and shook out these hidden meanings, and they were made manifest; the Prophets were shaken out, and the Apostles were born.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since then they were born of the Prophets who had been shaken out, the Apostles are sons of those that were shaken out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They, placed as the arrows in the hand of the giant, have reached the uttermost parts of the earth.…The Apostles the sons of the Prophets have been like as the arrows in the hand of a mighty one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He is mighty, He hath shaken them out with a mighty hand; if He hath shaken them out with a mighty hand, they whom He hath shaken forth have arrived even at the uttermost parts of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed is the man who hath filled his desire from them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Well, my brethren, who filleth his desire from them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who loveth not the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who is filled with the desire of the world, hath no room for that to enter which they have preached.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pour forth what thou carriest, and become fit for that which thou hast not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, thou desirest riches: thou canst not fill thy desire from them: thou desirest honours upon earth, thou desirest those things which God hath given even unto beasts of burden, that is, temporal pleasure, bodily health, and the like; thou wilt not fulfil thy desire from them.…“He shall not be ashamed, when he speaketh with his enemies in the gate.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, let us speak in the gate, that is, let all know what we speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he who chooseth not to speak in the gate, wisheth what he speaketh to be hidden, and perhaps wisheth it to be hidden for this reason, that it is evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he be confident, let him speak in the gate; as it is said of Wisdom, “She crieth at the gates, at the entry of the city.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As long as they hold unto righteousness in innocency, they shall not be ashamed: this is to preach at the gate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who is he who preacheth at the gate?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who preacheth in Christ; because Christ is the gate whereby we enter into that city.…They, therefore, who speak against Christ, are without the gate; because they seek their own honours, not those of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he who preacheth in the gate, seeketh Christ’s honour, not his own: and, therefore, he who preacheth in the gate, saith, Trust not in me; for ye will not enter through me, but through the gate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While they who wish men to trust in themselves, wish them not to enter through the gate: it is no marvel if the gate be closed against them, and if they vainly knock for it to be opened.
